publishedAbstract: - Multiclusterservice is only enabled for clusters configured with submariner globalnet.- When you have globalnet enabled via ACM Submariner add-on, it is required to add on the storagecluster `ocs-storagecluster` the network multiClusterService for globalnet. ~~~ spec: network: multiClusterService: clusterID: enabled: true ~~~- Enabling multilClusterService will export the mon and OSD service IPs to `exportedService` which is a globalenet resource.- This will trigger the creation of the service exports in the openshift-storage namespace, that can be seen with: `oc get serviceexport-n openshift-storage` ~~~ # oc get serviceexports.multicluster.x-k8s.io NAME AGE rook-ceph-mon-f 10d rook-ceph-mon-g 10d rook-ceph-mon-h 10d rook-ceph-osd-0 10d rook-ceph-osd-1 10d rook-ceph-osd-2 10d rook-ceph-osd-3 10d rook-ceph-osd-4 10d rook-ceph-osd-5 10d rook-ceph-osd-6 10d rook-ceph-osd-7 10d rook-ceph-osd-8 10d ~~~- If we remove / disable the globalnet, we need to remove also the multclusterservice network from the storagecluster, and that will break of course Regional DR, but also internal ceph communication that should not happen.- What should be the procedure for customers who want to uninstall the Regional DR Submariner add on with globalnet?
publishedTitle: Communication issues within the cluster after removal of network multiClusterService on ODF Regional DR
